title: podman-top: use containers/psgo
body:
Use github.com/containers/psgo instead of execing `ps (1)`.  The psgo
library enables a much more flexible interface with respect to which
data to be printed (e.g., capabilities, seccomp mode, PID, PCPU, etc.)
while the output can be parsed reliably.  The library does not use
ps (1) but parses /proc and /dev instead.  To list the processes of a
given container, psgo will join the mount namespace of the given
container and extract all data from there.

Notice that this commit breaks compatibility with docker-top.

Signed-off-by: Valentin Rothberg <vrothberg@suse.com>

comments.body: We should document the allowed format descriptors somewhere in the manpages, but the code LGTM
Assuming Travis is happy now, I'll commit ontop here to add bash-completion and add the flags to the manpage.
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably 98703eb) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
Updated the PR with latest containers/psgo, manpage changes, bash-completion adjustment to include the format descriptors etc.
Ok lets get this in.  We know we have some other issues, but we can fix them as psgo evolves.
